name: Flag Task
description: Lets the assistant flag useful follow-up work, then start each item in a new chat when you choose.

permissions.reason: Adds the flag_task tool the assistant can call.
Adds guidance for when the assistant should flag a follow-up.
Adds a command to review flagged follow-ups.
Attaches the current chat and workspace to each flagged task.
Keeps flagged tasks with this workspace.
Starts flagged tasks in new chats and opens started chats.
Sends the flagged task prompt into the new chat.
Shows how many follow-ups are waiting.
Confirms when a follow-up has been flagged.

rules.name: Flag useful follow-ups
rules.description: Adds guidance to flag concrete follow-up work discovered during a chat.

action.guidance: Follow-up hint: if you notice a concrete task that is useful but outside the user's current request, flag it with flag_task. If flag_task is not loaded, use search_tools to find the Flag Task extension tool first. Good cases include an unrelated bug, failing test, risky TODO, missing edge-case test, or docs drift. Skip vague ideas, already-requested work, and anything without evidence. After flagging, continue the current task.

tools.name: flag_task
tools.description: Flag a concrete follow-up task discovered while working, so the user can start it later in a new chat.

summary.description: What needs follow-up and why it matters.

reason.description: Why this should be handled separately from the current task.

priority.description: How important the follow-up appears to be.

evidence.description: Specific evidence, such as an error, test name, file, or observation.

suggested_prompt.description: The prompt to use when starting the follow-up in a new chat.

files.description: Relevant files or paths, if known.
